reason: Round 2 freeze used an asymmetric timed baseline (fused-baseline callable resolved INSIDE the CUDA-event timed closure while the candidate path was cached), inflating the baseline ~3us/small-shape. Geomean 1.0149x was not trustworthy. Do not use for any speedup decision.
note: kept for history only; replaced by id=baseline (commit 68a32061).

timing: symmetric: direct fused-baseline callable resolved once before the CUDA-event timed closure (Round 3 fix)
correctness: PASS: 10 production + 2400-case CI grid + 3 negative tests (correctness_prod.log 5 passed, cigrid_full.log 1 passed); oracle=split path, ATOL=8e-2/RTOL=1e-2
finding: Candidate routes to baseline; 0.9957x (~1.0x, slightly <1 from candidate's extra wrapper frame). Small shapes flat ~61us regardless of token count (dispatch floor); large shapes 59-96us.

named_bound.small: launch/dispatch-overhead bound: device 7.55us vs end-to-end 60.67us (~88% host dispatch/launch); kernel also tiny-grid (114<148 SMs, 0.10 waves/SM, 12.7% occupancy); DRAM read 249.6 KB
named_bound.large: memory-latency bound, NOT DRAM-bandwidth: 12.8% DRAM read %peak (5.2% write), long_scoreboard 11.9 ratio, 88.9% occupancy, compute SOL 60% / memory SOL 50%, L2 hit 50%

correction: Seed roofline assumed large=bandwidth-bound; NCU shows only ~13% DRAM peak -> latency-bound. Profiled, did not guess.
next: small: attack ~53us dispatch overhead via integrated install path (zero-overhead dispatcher) + PDL-off A/B (device kernel already ~7.55us); large: cos_sin_cache L2 reuse (hit only 50%) / load-latency, likely near-bound -> confirm vs candidate before any no-go.

ranked_directions: 1. integrated zero-overhead dispatch gate (small-dispatch) — only lever that moves small shapes (~53us host gap); NOT device code
2. CTA-per-token cos_sin shared staging (large-latency) — stage FP32 cos/sin once per token in smem, reuse across heads; ~5-15% if cos/sin loads dominate long_scoreboard
3. small-shape device launch-config (more CTAs/fewer warps) — only matters after #1
4. latency hiding / cache-policy in warp kernel (large) — 2-8% incremental
6. AVOID: split kernels, more PDL, TMA/TMEM (no credible fit)
large_verdict: not proven near-bound; sole credible lever is cos_sin L2 reuse via CTA-per-token smem staging; if it does not cut long_scoreboard/device-time, large is near the practical bound for this fused warp design
small_verdict: a native .cuh change cannot materially fix small shapes (~88% host dispatch); the only lever is the call path (integrated zero-overhead dispatcher), which fits 'native CUDA candidate' only if the candidate includes the jit/tvm-ffi installed dispatch route
no_go_risk: 65-75% that a pure device candidate yields geomean ~1.0x; lower only if integrated dispatcher removes the small-shape wrapper gap
decision: R4: establish workspace .cuh build path via load_jit (faithful baseline port) + PDL on/off A/B to de-risk the unproven build mechanism (AC-2 build + AC-4 first data; honest ~1.0x expected). R5: implement direction #2 (CTA-per-token cos_sin staging) on the validated build path; pursue direction #1 (integrated dispatcher) for small shapes.

kernel: src/qknorm_rope_candidate.cuh (workspace-owned; faithful port of sglang csrc/diffusion/qknorm_rope.cuh @6965fe0ee)
build: PASS: builds via SGLang load_jit (absolute cuda_files path) + make_cpp_args; cuda_wrappers QKNormRopeKernel<...>::run; no --use_fast_math; no torch.utils.cpp_extension
correctness: PASS: 10 production rows + 3 negatives on B200 (correctness_candidate.log: 5 passed)

isolated_benchmark_geomean.label: ARTIFACT — not a real device win

verdict: NO device speedup: the .cuh is a faithful port (identical SASS). The isolated 1.29-1.40x is an ASYMMETRIC-CALL-PATH artifact — the candidate calls the JIT module directly while the baseline goes through SGLang register_custom_op (~6-8us). Decomposition shows candidate-direct is ~0.79-0.95x vs baseline-direct (slightly SLOWER, from optimized_wrapper per-call Python: torch import + env read + dict lookup). Also HIGH run-to-run variance on the shared B200 (same baseline custom-op path: 60.9us in benchmark.py vs 21.9us in the quieter decomposition). Do NOT promote.
next: R5: (a) make optimized_wrapper lean (resolve torch/pdl/config once, not per call); (b) judge device speed via the INTEGRATED install path (kda_kernels.install behind the public custom op) so candidate and baseline share the SAME wrapper layer, with interleaved sampling vs clock/contention variance; (c) implement direction #2 (CTA-per-token cos_sin shared staging) as the real large-shape device lever.

kernel: src/qknorm_rope_candidate.cuh QKNormRopeStagedKernel (fused_qknorm_rope_cta_token): one CTA per token, cos/sin row staged once into shared mem and reused across the token's q+k heads; per-head RMS-norm+RoPE math identical to baseline
build: via SGLang load_jit + make_cpp_args + cache_once; flags -DSGL_CUDA_ARCH=1000 -std=c++20 -O3 --expt-relaxed-constexpr (NO --use_fast_math; build_flags.log); selected by KDA_CAND_VARIANT=staged
correctness: PASS: 10 production rows + 3 negatives on B200 (correctness_staged.log: 5 passed)
device_fair_geomean: 1.0787x interleaved (warp-variant sanity = 0.9994x confirms the device-fair comparison is fair, resolving the R4 asymmetry artifact)
decomposition: profile/decompose.py + decompose.log: custom-op overhead ~4-5us; device-fair large 1.15x, small 0.86x (staging slightly hurts tiny-grid small shapes)
verdict: REAL device win on the LARGE bucket (NCU-confirmed long_scoreboard reduction); NO win on small (tiny-grid + host-dispatch-bound). Justifies a per-bucket dispatcher (large->staged, small->warp/baseline). Production claim still needs integrated install-path validation (symmetric custom-op for both).
next: R6: integrated install-path validation of the large win + evidence-gated dispatcher (large->staged, small->baseline) + docs/dispatch.md; then AC-7 ledger finalize + AC-8 export.

what: src/register.py optimized_wrapper is now a deterministic evidence-gated per-bucket dispatcher (no env dependence)

routes.non-production / non-contiguous: SGLang baseline fallback before C++ matcher

correctness: PASS with dispatcher active on B200: 6 passed (production rows route large->staged + small->baseline; 3 negatives; dispatch-logic unit test); correctness_dispatch.log
device_fair_geomean: 1.0787x (large 1.10-1.26x via staged, small ~1.0x via baseline; warp-sanity 0.9994x)
integrated_path_attempt: benchmark.py --integrated (register_custom_op-wrapped, integrated.log) is CONFOUNDED: the small/baseline route re-enters the baseline's OWN register_custom_op inside the candidate custom op (double-wrap -> artificial ~0.72x small) and ran in a high-variance window -> geomean 0.86x is NOT a valid metric and is not claimed. In production kda_kernels.install() replaces the public op (no double layer) so small->baseline=1.0x; clean integrated validation deferred to the AC-8 export (kda_kernels plain-dispatcher path).
verdict: Dispatcher wires the verified large device win into the public path; small unchanged. Production device estimate = device-fair 1.0787x -- a real, defensible win (not a no-go).
next: R7: AC-7 finalize (interface.md final signature/dispatch/fallback/source-lineage incl commit 56997201e); then AC-8 SGLang export + in-SGLang correctness/smoke/fallback + kda_kernels integrated validation.

correction: The 1.0793x here was a PROXY (benchmark.py --integrated timing the original baseline custom-op vs optimized_wrapper called DIRECTLY), NOT the literal kda_kernels.install() overlay path. Round 8 ran the real install path (export + install + time the INSTALLED public symbol) and measured geomean 0.9301x/0.9185x -- a NET REGRESSION. The proxy understated the overlay's per-call Python dispatch tax (generated dispatcher + wrapper frame ~7us > the baseline's C-level register_custom_op). The large-bucket DEVICE win is real (device-fair 1.0679x R8) but does not survive the overlay. See id=export_r8.
what: Exact-shape fail-closed dispatcher + faithful one-layer integrated benchmark
dispatcher: optimized_wrapper routes ONLY the 5 large captured (tokens,heads) rows -> staged (with full bf16/float32-cache/int64-positions/contiguity checks); the 5 small rows + non-captured + non-production + non-contiguous -> SGLang baseline; the public callable reads NO env (KDA_CAND_VARIANT and KDA_CAND_PDL removed)
correctness: PASS on B200: 9 passed (-k 'not ci_grid': production + 3 negatives + exact-shape routing + fail-closed gate + fallback-correctness B1000/H24+B4096/H16+int32-pos + invalid-env); 1 passed for the full 2400 CI grid (correctness-or-fallback) -- correctness_r7.log / cigrid_r7.log

ledger: local benchmark.csv reconciled = R3 frozen baseline (GEOMEAN_production 0.9957x) + R7 integrated (GEOMEAN_integrated 1.0793x); docs/dispatch.md decision table updated; integrated.log/integrated_r7.log + correctness logs in REMOTE_KDA_DIR
verdict: Net production win (geomean 1.0793x) driven by large-bucket staging (device-fair 1.10-1.26x, NCU-confirmed; integrated 1.21-1.28x). Small bucket has an honest ~6% regression from the dispatcher frame (dispatch-bound). Promotable for the large-token diffusion shapes; small left on the proven baseline.
next: R8: AC-8 SGLang export (.cuh under python/sglang/jit_kernel/csrc, preserve public fused_inplace_qknorm_rope, in-SGLang correctness + smoke + fallback) + docs/sglang_jit_export.md; optionally make the dispatcher not intercept small (avoid the ~6% frame regression).

what: Made the candidate exportable (split src/register.py thin + src/wrapper.py impl + EXPORTS, recursion-safe baseline capture, full fail-closed gate), ran the REAL repo export + the LITERAL kda_kernels.install() path, and validated AC-4 + AC-8 on the production overlay. Result is a net regression -> evidence-backed no-go; NOT promoted.
export: scripts/export_kda_kernels/export.py b200_diffusion_qknorm_rope__multi_shape generates kda_kernels/diffusion/qknorm_rope/{_dispatcher.py,_impls/b200/*}, flips KDA_OPTIMIZED_fused_inplace_qknorm_rope=True; read_exports() returns {fused_inplace_qknorm_rope} (register.py execs with no __file__). Mirrors the promoted h200 PR#19 tvm-ffi structure.
dropin_correctness: PASS on B200 (test_install_path_dropin_and_no_recursion, KDA_RUN_INTEGRATED=1): kda_kernels.install(force=True,strict=True) reports 'swapped'; the INSTALLED public symbol routes a large captured row -> staged CUDA (dispatch 'cuda', oracle match) and small + int32 rows -> the captured ORIGINAL baseline (dispatch 'fallback', oracle match, NO recursion); baseline restored on uninstall. correctness_r8.log: 10 passed (-k 'not ci_grid'); cigrid_r8.log: full 2400 grid 1 passed.
install_path_geomean: 0.9301x (run1) / 0.9185x (run2) -- NET REGRESSION. Per-shape (run1): joyai-edit B7904/H32 1.2094x (only winner), qwen B4096 0.9715x, qwen-edit B8424 0.9980x, zimage B4096 0.9289x, zimage B4128 0.9250x, all 5 small 0.85-0.87x. benchmark.csv *__install rows + GEOMEAN_install.
device_fair_geomean: 1.0679x (R8 re-run, same session): large 1.10-1.26x (joyai 1.26, qwen 1.14, qwen-edit 1.19, zimage 1.11/1.10), small 0.98-1.00x; warp faithful-port sanity 0.9999x confirms the device-fair comparison is fair. The staged DEVICE win is REAL and NCU-confirmed (R5: B8424 device 109.6->88.1us, long_scoreboard 11.9->9.29).
named_bound: Production active bound = HOST-SIDE DISPATCH/LAUNCH overhead on this small-per-call, latency-bound kernel. The device-fair (1.0679x, symmetric direct-JIT) vs literal-install (0.9301x) GAP is the kda_kernels overlay per-call Python dispatch tax (~7us > the baseline's C-level register_custom_op). Only joyai (device save ~18us) overcomes it; the other 4 large shapes (save 4.5-11us) land parity-to-loss and the 5 dispatch-bound small shapes (no device save, ~9.7us device vs ~65us end-to-end) eat the full tax. Equal-weight geomean over the 10 rows is therefore <1 on the production path.
decision: NOT PROMOTED. Reverted the export (kda_kernels overlay restored to the un-promoted stub, KDA_OPTIMIZED=False) so installing does not ship a regression. The export-ready src/ + full evidence remain; re-running export.py reproduces the overlay. This is a well-supported evidence-backed no-go (frozen baseline R3 + real candidate attempts R4/R5 + NCU evidence R5 + named bound + literal install measurement R8).
recommendation: The staged cos/sin device optimization would only become a net win if the kda_kernels overlay had a near-zero-overhead (C-level) dispatch path, OR if these diffusion qknorm_rope calls were issued under CUDA graphs (out of scope: single-op, no-CUDA-graph per the plan). Within this task's boundary the candidate is a no-go.

what: Re-did the integration the #19 (h200 qknorm_rope) way: REAL in-tree placement of the candidate .cuh inside SGLang's csrc, keeping SGLang's OWN register_custom_op wrapper. This is torch.compile-SAFE (the public op stays a registered custom op), unlike the kda_kernels overlay (plain dispatcher) which the user requires for torch.compile compatibility.
cuh_change: src/qknorm_rope_candidate.cuh: QKNormRopeKernel<...>::run now delegates the EXACT production template (head_dim=128, rope_dim=128, !is_neox, bf16) with num_tokens>=kStagedMinTokens(512) to QKNormRopeStagedKernel (the CTA-per-token cos/sin staging device win); all other templates/small shapes use the warp path unchanged. constexpr-gated so the CI grid's non-production configs are untouched. So a plain swap of THIS .cuh into SGLang's csrc/diffusion/qknorm_rope.cuh transparently delivers the win via SGLang's own load_jit + register_custom_op.
validation: B200 (ion-b200 GPU4), isolated SGLang worktree at the box HEAD (0b65588c1); candidate .cuh dropped into python/sglang/jit_kernel/csrc/diffusion/qknorm_rope.cuh; SGLang's OWN public fused_inplace_qknorm_rope built it via load_jit. profile/in_sglang/validate_in_tree.py.
correctness: 10/10 production shapes oracle_ok through SGLang's real op (vs split oracle, ATOL=8e-2/RTOL=1e-2); no NaN. (intree_cand.json / intree_base.json in REMOTE_KDA_DIR).
perf: In-tree device geomean ~1.07-1.12x, torch.compile-safe (both candidate and baseline go through SGLang's identical register_custom_op, so the ratio is the pure DEVICE delta): large 1.10-1.33x (joyai/qwen/qwen-edit/zimage), small ~1.0x PARITY (warp kernel byte-identical to baseline). Device-fair (interleaved, noise-canceling) = 1.0679x is the conservative robust number; in-tree run2 (back-to-back warm) = 1.1182x. NOTE: in-tree run1 small shapes mis-measured at ~0.60x due to cross-process variance (separate-process measurement can't interleave); physically impossible (identical kernel) and corrected by run2 + the device-fair parity.
torch_compile: SAFE: SGLang's register_custom_op wrapper is preserved; only the device .cuh is swapped. Contrast: the kda_kernels overlay (id=export_r8/lean) replaces the public symbol with a PLAIN dispatcher, dropping register_custom_op -> NOT torch.compile-safe; that overlay path is NOT promoted (user requires torch.compile).
decision: Ship the in-tree placement (torch.compile-safe device win); do NOT promote the kda_kernels overlay. Mirrors the promoted h200 #19 AC-13 in-tree placement.

perf: GEOMEAN_devfair 1.0648x (baseline/ copy vs staged, interleaved; large 1.0818-1.2458x, small 0.9862-1.0013x parity) — reproduces prior-round 1.0679x on the new stack. GEOMEAN_devfair_sglbase 1.0701x (upstream sglang jit module base) — copy==upstream within noise. GEOMEAN_pdlab 1.0035x (staged PDL-ON vs PDL-OFF; mixed per-row signs) — PDL neutral. caveat: __devfair_warp lane (1.0606x) is NOT a fairness sanity anymore: the candidate .cuh's QKNormRopeKernel::run delegates production-template num_tokens>=kStagedMinTokens(512) to the staged kernel (verified in src/qknorm_rope_candidate.cuh), so the warp-class request still runs staged on all 5 large rows. Copy-equivalence (sglbase vs copy geomean delta 0.5%) + small-row parity serve as the controls instead.
decision: KEEP the staged CTA-per-token kernel as the candidate; PDL stays arch-default ON (A/B neutral within noise; flipping would diverge from SGLang jit_kernel conventions for <0.4% sub-noise). Next: fresh NCU bound closure (profile/r9_staged_b200/), then in-SGLang in-tree arbiter.

what: Bounded exploration probe (the single NCU-justified direction per DEC-2): two-token-per-CTA staged variant (fused_qknorm_rope_cta_token2 / QKNormRopeStaged2Kernel). Hypothesis from NCU r9: amortize the per-token staging barriers (~7.5% of pcsamp) and double independent q/k load streams per block (long_scoreboard on the load-consumption line = 49% of pcsamp).
validation: ion-b200 GPU phys 1 (idle), SGLang 0b65588c1, task commit 355f3bf2a, same session as the staged rerun for head-to-head comparability.
correctness: 10/10 captured rows oracle_ok module-direct (check_staged2.py; isfinite + ATOL=8e-2/RTOL=1e-2).
perf: GEOMEAN_devfair_staged2 = 1.0658x vs baseline/ copy; same-session staged rerun GEOMEAN_devfair = 1.0691x (earlier run 1.0648x). staged2 is parity-to-slightly-worse: joyai B7904/H32 72.59us vs staged 70.64us (-2.7%), qwen B4096 42.91 vs 42.08 (-2.0%), B8424 equal (69.63 vs 69.68), zimage equal, small rows parity.
why: At waves/SM=1.0 with 8 resident CTAs/SM, inter-CTA parallelism already hides intra-CTA staging barriers; interleaving two tokens adds smem footprint (2x rows) and pair-tail effects without increasing useful memory-level parallelism — the q/k loads were already latency-limited by mandatory traffic, not by per-CTA issue slots.
decision: REJECT staged2; close bounded exploration. Within the <=2-iteration budget a second intra-CTA variant has no remaining NCU rationale (same mechanism). The staged anchor stands as the operating point (consistent with the sibling H200 row-norm finding that structural variants lose at the shipping layer, and with TRT-LLM PR-13052/PR-11869 which use the same single-pass CTA-smem design).
evidence: benchmark.csv *__devfair_staged2 rows + GEOMEAN_devfair_staged2 @ 355f3bf2a; REMOTE_KDA_DIR/logs/devfair_staged2.log + devfair_staged_rerun.log; profile/r9_staged_b200/analysis/ (stall hotspots, compare table); KernelWiki pages pr-TensorRT-LLM-13052, pr-TensorRT-LLM-11869, pr-sglang-15141

what: Final shipping-integration arbiter on the jit_kernel/tvm-ffi stack: candidate .cuh (sha 874b1bfa) swapped into an isolated SGLang worktree at 0b65588c1 (register_custom_op byte-unchanged); per-side TVM_FFI_CACHE_DIR isolation; alternating sides; run1 discarded (warm), run2 recorded, run1 as regression cross-check; enforced gate (>3% material per-shape regression, geomean >= 1.0, correctness).
perf: GEOMEAN_intree_r9 = 1.0970x over 10 captured shapes. Large: joyai 1.2087, qwen 1.2480, qwen-edit 1.1410, zimage 1.2575/1.2714. Small: 0.9733-0.9908 (22.2-22.8us both sides; <3%, run1-confirmed not material). PROMOTION_GATE PASS.
correctness: 10/10 oracle_ok through SGLang's real public op in all 4 measure runs; torch.compile fullgraph smoke PASS both sides (small+large captured + broad synthetic B1024/H16, compiled==eager); SGLang's own test_qknorm_rope.py FULL grid 1248 passed inside the candidate worktree (broad staged surface: all bf16/128/128 non-neox >=512-token combos).
decomposition: Public custom-op layer ~7-8us/call on BOTH sides (identical layers, cancels in ratio); in-tree path adds no Python. Single-input-set protocol runs faster in absolute us than the two-set devfair lane (L2 residency of in-place q/k) — same-protocol ratios are the admissible evidence.
review: Pre-arbiter independent review (gpt-5.5) required: cache isolation, broad-surface coverage, enforced materiality gate, PDL rule reconciliation, run1+run2 preservation — all implemented before this run (commit f46075e69).
decision: PROMOTE the in-tree placement (same mechanism as the prior round, now with valid tvm-ffi-stack evidence). The ../../docs/tvm_ffi_benchmark_status.md Blocked row for b200 qknorm_rope is cleared by this evidence. Upstream SGLang PR remains out of task scope (validation-scoped worktree only).
